clinical_coherence.notes: 74M African American with DM2, HTN, BPH, hearing loss. Progressive memory difficulties over 18 months. MMSE 21/30. Clinical picture coherent. However, PMH contains BPH and tamsulosin -- similar template to S03 and S04. The hearing loss as a complicating factor for cognitive assessment is a nice touch.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities concordant. MRI shows bilateral hippocampal atrophy (Scheltens 2-3) with temporoparietal volume loss and Fazekas grade 2 white matter changes (consistent with vascular risk factors). Neuropsych confirms amnestic profile. Amyloid PET positive. CSF A+T+N+. All consistent with early AD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G30.1 correct. Critical actions comprehensive. Differential appropriately considers mixed AD/vascular dementia given Fazekas 2 white matter changes and vascular risk factors. Good inclusion of hearing loss as a modifiable dementia risk factor.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately straightforward. Classic amnestic presentation. The Fazekas 2 white matter changes add mild complexity (mixed pathology consideration) but the diagnosis is still clear. Slightly more nuanced than S01.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 74M African American with DM2 and HTN is a realistic AD risk profile. African Americans have approximately 2x the AD risk compared to non-Hispanic whites. Hearing loss is a known modifiable dementia risk factor. However, the PMH template (DM2, HTN, BPH, tamsulosin, metformin, amlodipine, losartan, aspirin) is shared with S03 and S04.

educational_value.notes: Good case covering AD evaluation in an African American male with vascular comorbidities. Teaches about mixed AD/vascular pathology consideration. Hearing loss as dementia risk factor is a valuable teaching point. Some redundancy with S01 in core teaching.

issues_found: PMH template shared with S03 and S04
strengths: Hearing loss as complicating factor and modifiable risk
